content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Major architectural facts I missed in the prior version:

1. **Passthrough optimization**: vsim's `StateDrain` only drains GPRs+PC+(V-CSRs when V) via DM. FP/M-CSRs/PMP/V-regfile flow through unchanged from QEMU 1's snapshot.
2. **`ResumeDriver` is selective**: only restores GPRs, DPC, `mtvec`, and (when V is enabled) the V-CSRs. M-mode CSRs/PMP/FP are never written into the vsim hart.
3. **Three trigger forms**: ENTER, EXIT, RUNTIME (`csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` decided by rs1's runtime value).
4. **Tier-A Abstract-first with PB fallback**: `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` retries via Program Buffer when the DM rejects the abstract write.
5. **V regfile read-back happens in the simulator wrapper**, not StateDrain - keeps DMI separated from mmap.
6. **`rt_c_v_csrs`** is a separate fixture for V-CSR survival.

Rewriting the doc.

input.content:
# How the Hybrid Simulator Works A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. ## The Problem in One Paragraph A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. ## High-Level Concept Five big ideas make this possible: 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. ## State Categories and Their Transport The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. ## Trigger Modes Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. ## Design Rationale **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. ## Architecture Flowchart ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] end subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN end subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG V_TRIG --> V_CTRL V_CTRL --> V_DM end subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG end Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM ``` ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. ### What each phase does ``` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 ``` ### State that crosses each handoff | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | ### What each step proves | Step | What it tests | |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. ## Where to Look Next - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. ## Mental Model in One Sentence > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. ## Architecture Flowchart ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] SHM[("/dev/shm RAM

Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. ### What each phase does ``` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 ``` ### State that crosses each handoff | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | ### What each step proves | Step | What it tests | |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. ## Where to Look Next - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. ## Mental Model in One Sentence > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

lines: ## High-Level Concept
-Four big ideas make this possible:
+Five big ideas make this possible:

-1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant - no transfer cost.

+1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant.

-2. **Architectural CPU state is small enough to copy through one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs, the V regfile (`v0..v31`), and V scalar CSRs (`vtype`, `vl`, `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`) are all described by `struct hybrid_state_v1` in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on, written to a file at each handoff.

+2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on.

-3. **The trigger is a magic CSR write.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` = "QEMU, hand off to vsim". `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` = "vsim, hand back to QEMU". CSR `0x7C0` lives in the user-defined range so it cannot collide with anything real.

+3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff.

-4. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording measurements.

+4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI.
+5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording.
## State Categories and Their Transport
-The wire struct is uniform, but each category travels through a different host primitive depending on what QEMU and the RTL Debug Module are willing to expose.
+The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose.

-| Category                                                | Out of QEMU 1                       | Into vsim                                                | Out of vsim                | Into QEMU 2                |

-| GPRs, FPRs, scalar CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, ...)        | TCG plugin reads via `qemu_plugin_read_register()` | DM Abstract Commands                              | DM Abstract Commands       | gdbstub `G` / `P` packets  |

-| V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl`                           | TCG plugin                          | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6`            | DM Abstract Commands       | gdbstub `P` packets        |

-| V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin                   | DM Abstract Commands                                     | DM Abstract Commands       | gdbstub `P` packets        |

-| V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB)                        | TCG plugin                          | scratch region in shared mmap; fixture executes `vle8.v` | fixture executes `vse8.v`; vsim reads RAM bytes | gdbstub `P` packets |

-| Guest memory                                            | not copied                          | not copied                                               | not copied                 | not copied                 |
+| Category                                          | QEMU 1 -> snapshot   | snapshot -> vsim hart                                | vsim hart -> snapshot                       | snapshot -> QEMU 2 |
+| GPRs `x1..x31`, PC                                | TCG plugin reads     | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC)                    | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read)           | gdbstub `G` packet |
+| `mtvec`                                           | TCG plugin           | DM Abstract Command                                  | passthrough (not re-read)                   | gdbstub `P`        |

+| Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written)                            | passthrough                                 | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) |

+| FPRs `f0..f31`                                    | TCG plugin           | passthrough                                          | passthrough                                 | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) |

+| PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr)                            | TCG plugin           | passthrough                                          | passthrough                                 | gdbstub `P` (each slot) |
+| V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl`                     | TCG plugin           | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6`        | DM Abstract Command (fresh read)            | gdbstub `P`        |

+| V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read)            | gdbstub `P`        |

+| V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB)                  | TCG plugin           | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) |
+| `vlenb`                                           | TCG plugin           | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN)          | passthrough (verified, not re-read)         | not sent           |

+| Guest memory                                      | -- (in shared mmap)  | -- (in shared mmap)                                  | -- (in shared mmap)                         | -- (in shared mmap) |
-Memory does not appear in the wire struct because shared mmap removes it from the transport problem.
+"Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2.
+## Trigger Modes
+Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`.
+| Mode | Entry into vsim                                | Exit out of vsim                              | Source of truth |
+| CSR  | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself |
+| PC   | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match)    | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged |
+Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`):
+- `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire.
+- `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT`  - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC.
+- `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime.
## Design Rationale

-**One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth.

+**One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2).

**QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`.

-**Most registers go through DM Abstract Commands; `vtype` and `vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV Debug Module - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Debug Module's Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage one instruction, run it on the halted core, then continue with abstract commands for the rest.

+**Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight.

-**The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 × 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain is the mirror: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch before the exit trigger, and vsim memcpy's those bytes into `state.vreg[]`. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory, with no new transport channel.

+**The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage.

-**Into QEMU 2, the V regfile reaches the target through gdbstub `P` packets.** Gdbstub already speaks RV-V via the standard XML target description, so writing `v0..v31` is just one `P` packet per V-reg index after the `G` packet for GPRs. No new mechanism is needed on the QEMU 2 side.

+**Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore.

+**The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory.

+**Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level.

## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test

-The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip.

+The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip.
Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap:
### State that crosses each handoff

-| Boundary           | Memory state                                             | GPR / scalar CSR state                              | V regfile state                                                      | V scalar CSR state                                       |

-| QEMU 1 -> vsim     | `SEED` already filled by Phase 1; vsim sees it via mmap | drained by plugin, restored by DM Abstract Commands | unused by Phase 1 (zero on the wire)                                 | `mstatus.VS` re-enabled, `vsetvl` staged via Program Buffer |

-| vsim -> QEMU 2     | `SEED` unchanged; mutated `v0..v31` materialised at `V_SCRATCH` by `vse8.v` | drained by Abstract Commands, written via gdbstub `G` | scratch bytes copied into `state.vreg[]` by drain, then sent as gdbstub `P` packets per V-reg | drained, re-applied by gdbstub `P` packets |

+| Boundary           | Memory                                                       | Fresh-read in vsim             | Passthrough through vsim                                | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) |

+| QEMU 1 -> vsim     | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly         | (nothing yet)                  | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH |

+| in vsim            | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH         | --                             | --                                                      | --                              |

+| vsim -> QEMU 2     | `SEED` unchanged                                             | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM)     | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg |
### What each step proves
-| Step                                       | What it tests                                                                                  |
-| Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores      | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping).                     |
-| `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim     | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). |

-| Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass            | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init. |
-| Final exit code 0                          | Full round-trip succeeded for memory, GPRs, V regfile, and V-CSRs.                             |

+| Step                                         | What it tests                                                                                                                                                |

+| Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores        | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping).                                                                                    |

+| `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim       | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). |

+| Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass              | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. |

+| Final exit code 0                            | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2.                         |

-If `vle32` traps, `mstatus.VS` is wrong. If the regfile verification fails for `v9` only, the V scratch path lost the mutation. If GPRs are wrong, `t0` (holding the SEED address) was not restored before the asm executed.
+A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic.
## Where to Look Next
- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract.
-- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` and the entry-trigger callback.
-- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/FPR/CSR + V-CSR restore order.
-- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM Abstract Command capture.
-- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain.
-- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2 + RSP `G`/`P` packets.
-- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c` - the worked example above.
+- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers.
+- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub.
+- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures.
- `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest.
## Mental Model in One Sentence
-> Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write as the baton-pass; vector regs ride along inside that shared memory.

+> Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

content.text:
Updated `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` to match the actual current architecture. Concrete corrections vs the prior version:

- **Passthrough optimization (was wrong before).** vsim does NOT round-trip every register through DM. `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs; FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), PMP, and `vlenb` flow from the QEMU 1 snapshot straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore unchanged. Added a dedicated rationale paragraph and a column for "passthrough" in every relevant table.
- **`ResumeDriver` is selective.** Only writes `x1..x31`, DPC, `mtvec`, and (when V) the V-CSRs. The other M-mode CSRs are never written into the vsim hart - documented this explicitly.
- **Tier-A V-CSR Abstract-with-PB-fallback path.** Mentioned `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` and the per-CSR fallback semantics.
- **Three trigger kinds + two trigger modes.** Added a "Trigger Modes" section covering `HYBRID_HANDOFF_{ENTER,EXIT,RUNTIME}` and the CSR-vs-PC mode split (plugin `enter_pc=` arg / vsim `--hybrid-exit-pc` CLI).
- **Missing-register tolerance.** New rationale paragraph on `try_write_named` skip and DM `cmderr`-tolerant reads.
- **Flowchart**: added the `HandoffController` node showing the passthrough merge, the `add_pc_hit` trigger source, and the V-scratch stage/drain arrows.
- **`rt_c_v_csrs` fixture** added as a sibling example for V scalar CSR survival.
- **"Where to Look Next"** updated with `handoff_controller.hpp` and `program_buffer.hpp`.

#=========================================================================
# crt0.S : Entry point for RISC-V user programs
#=========================================================================

#ifndef CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
#define CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS 0
#endif

#define mcache_ctl 0x7ca
#define mmisc_ctl 0x7d0

  .text
  .global _start
  .type   _start, @function
_start:
  # Enable icache
  csrsi   mcache_ctl, 0x1

#ifdef ENABLE_DCACHE_COHERENCE
  # Enable dcache coherence management
  li      a0, 1 << 19
  csrs    mcache_ctl, a0
1:
  csrr    a0, mcache_ctl
#if __nds_v5
  bbc     a0, 20, 1b
#else
  srli    a1, a0, 20
  andi    a1, a1, 1
  beqz    a1, 1b
#endif
#endif

  # Enable d-cache and hardware prefetch
  ori     a0, a0, 0x703
  csrw    mcache_ctl, a0

  .weak   _stack
  # Initialize stack pointer (if defined)
  lla     a0, _stack
  beqz    a0, 1f
  mv      sp, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  slli    a0, a0, 12
  sub     sp, sp, a0
#endif

  # Initialize global pointer
.option push
.option norelax
1:lla gp, __global_pointer$
.option pop

#ifdef __nds_v5
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg
  bbc     a0, 3, 1f
  la      a1, _ITB_BASE_
  csrrw   x0, uitb, a1
1:
#if __riscv_xlen >= 64
  bbc     a0, 52, 1f
#else
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  csrr    a0, mmsc_cfg2
  bbc     a0, 20, 1f
#endif
  csrr    a0, mrvarch_cfg
  bbc     a0, 31, 1f
  la      a0, __jvt_base$
  csrw    jvt, a0
#endif
1:

  # Set mstatus.fs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 13)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Set mstatus.vs to 1 (initial)
  li      a0, (1 << 10)
  csrs    mstatus, a0

  # Enable unaligned memory access (mmisc_ctl.UNA)
  li      a0, (1 << 6)
  csrs    mmisc_ctl, a0

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss

  li      a1, 0xa55a
.Lother_hart_wait_loop:
  ld      a0, other_hart_boot_flag
  bne     a0, a1, .Lother_hart_wait_loop
  j       .Lmain
#else
  csrr    a0, mhartid
  beqz    a0, .Lclear_bss
  li      a0, 0x01234571
  li      a1, 0xe0080000
  sw      a0, 0(a1)
1:
  j       1b
#endif

  # Clear the bss segment
.Lclear_bss:
  la      a0, _edata
  la      a2, _end
  sub     a2, a2, a0
  li      a1, 0
  call    memset


#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  li      a0, 0xa55a
  lla     a1, other_hart_boot_flag
  sd      a0, 0(a1)
  fence
#endif

  call init_mtvec

.Lmain:
  li      a0, 0
  li      a1, 0
  li      a2, 0
  call    main

  call    _exit

.Lloop:
  j       .Lloop

  .size   _start, .-_start

#if CRT0_MULTIPLE_HARTS
  .data
  .p2align 3
  .global other_hart_boot_flag
  .type   other_hart_boot_flag, @object
other_hart_boot_flag:
  .8byte 0
  .size other_hart_boot_flag, . - other_hart_boot_flag
#endif

/*
 * Copyright (c) 2017 Andes Technology Corporation
 * All rights reserved.
 *
 * This file defines the reset/nmi/trap handlers, the reset initialization code
 * and the trap entry table for Vectored-PLIC.
 *
 * Please note that the code in this file is to facilitate AndesCore processor
 * verification and it is NOT a goal for this code to be used in production.
 * For sample production version of start.S, please find it in Andes Startup
 * demos.
 */

#include "core_v5.h"

	.section .text
	.align 6

.global m_trap_entry
m_trap_entry:
	addi sp, sp, -40*REGBYTES

	/* Save caller registers */
	STORE x0, 0*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x1, 1*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x3, 3*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x4, 4*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x5, 5*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x6, 6*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x7, 7*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x8, 8*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x9, 9*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x10, 10*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x11, 11*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x12, 12*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x13, 13*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x14, 14*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x15, 15*REGBYTES(sp)
#ifndef __riscv_32e
	STORE x16, 16*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x17, 17*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x18, 18*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x19, 19*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x20, 20*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x21, 21*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x22, 22*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x23, 23*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x24, 24*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x25, 25*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x26, 26*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x27, 27*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x28, 28*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x29, 29*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x30, 30*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE x31, 31*REGBYTES(sp)
#endif

	/* Save mstatus and mepc */
	csrr t0, mepc
	csrr t1, mstatus
	csrr t2, mxstatus

	STORE t0, 32*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE t1, 33*REGBYTES(sp)
	STORE t2, 34*REGBYTES(sp)

	/* Call trap handler */
	csrr a0, mcause
	mv   a1, sp
	call handle_trap

	/* Disable interrupt */
	csrrci a5, mstatus, MSTATUS_MIE

	/* Restore mstatus and mepc */
	LOAD t0, 32*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD t1, 33*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD t2, 34*REGBYTES(sp)

	csrw mepc, t0
	csrw mstatus, t1
	csrw mxstatus, t2

	/* Restore caller registers */
	LOAD x0, 0*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x1, 1*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x3, 3*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x4, 4*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x5, 5*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x6, 6*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x7, 7*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x8, 8*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x9, 9*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x10, 10*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x11, 11*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x12, 12*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x13, 13*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x14, 14*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x15, 15*REGBYTES(sp)
#ifndef __riscv_32e
	LOAD x16, 16*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x17, 17*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x18, 18*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x19, 19*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x20, 20*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x21, 21*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x22, 22*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x23, 23*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x24, 24*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x25, 25*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x26, 26*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x27, 27*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x28, 28*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x29, 29*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x30, 30*REGBYTES(sp)
	LOAD x31, 31*REGBYTES(sp)
#endif

	addi sp, sp, 40*REGBYTES
	mret

#ifndef HTIF_H
#define HTIF_H

#include <stddef.h>
#include <stdint.h>

// L1 Cache Control Commands
#define CCTL_L1D_WBINVAL_ALL 6
#define CCTL_L1D_WB_ALL      7
#define CCTL_L1D_INVAL_ALL   23

// L2 Cache Control and Status Constants
#define CCTL_L2_WBINVAL_ALL  0x12
#define CCTL_L2_ST_RUNNING   1
#define CCTL_ST_MASK         0xF

// L2 Cache Control Register Addresses
#define L2C_REG_CTL          0xe0500008
#define L2C_REG_CCTL         0xe0500040
#define L2C_REG_CCTL_M0_ST   0xe0500080
#define TOHOST_CMD_REG       0xe0080010

#if __riscv_xlen == 64
#define TOHOST_CMD(dev, cmd, payload) \
  (((uint64_t)(dev) << 56) | ((uint64_t)(cmd) << 48) | (uint64_t)(payload))
#define REG_T uint64_t
#else
#define TOHOST_CMD(dev, cmd, payload) \
  ({ \
    if ((dev) || (cmd)) \
      __builtin_trap(); \
    (payload); \
  })
#define REG_T uint32_t
#endif

static inline void flush_all_dcache(void)
{
  // L1 DCache writeback and invalidate all
  asm volatile("csrw mcctlcommand, %0" :: "rK"(CCTL_L1D_WBINVAL_ALL));

#if __riscv_xlen == 64
  // Check for L2 cache and flush it if available
  asm volatile(
    "csrr    t0, mmsc_cfg\n"
    "li      t1, 1\n"
    "slli    t1, t1, 46\n"
    "and     t2, t0, t1\n"
    "beqz    t2, 1f\n"
    "li      t3, 0xe0500008\n"
    "ld      t4, 0(t3)\n"
    "andi    t4, t4, 1\n"
    "beqz    t4, 1f\n"
    "li      t5, 0xe0500040\n"
    "li      t6, %[cmd]\n"
    "sd      t6, 0(t5)\n"
    "2:\n"
    "li      t2, 0xe0500080\n"
    "ld      t3, 0(t2)\n"
    "li      t4, %[mask]\n"
    "and     t3, t3, t4\n"
    "li      t5, %[running]\n"
    "beq     t3, t5, 2b\n"
    "1:\n"
    :
    : [cmd] "K"(CCTL_L2_WBINVAL_ALL),
      [mask] "K"(CCTL_ST_MASK),
      [running] "K"(CCTL_L2_ST_RUNNING)
  );
#endif
}

__attribute__((used)) static inline void sim_syscall(uint64_t dev,
                                                       uint64_t cmd,
                                                       uint64_t tohost)
{
  volatile uint64_t* command = (volatile uint64_t*) TOHOST_CMD_REG;
  uint64_t tohost_cmd = TOHOST_CMD(dev, cmd, tohost);
  flush_all_dcache();
  *command = tohost_cmd;
  asm volatile("fence rw,rw");
}

__attribute__((used)) static inline uint64_t frontend_syscall(uint64_t n,
                                                               uint64_t a0,
                                                               uint64_t a1,
                                                               uint64_t a2,
                                                               uint64_t a3,
                                                               uint64_t a4,
                                                               uint64_t a5,
                                                               uint64_t a6)
{
  volatile uint64_t magicmem[8] = {n, a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6};
  sim_syscall(0, 0, (uintptr_t) magicmem);
  return magicmem[0];
}

#endif // HTIF_H

1	/* Drain markers and fail-fast helper shared by all C-level round-trip
2	 * fixtures (rt_c_hello.c, rt_c_printf.c, rt_c_file_io.c).
3	 *
4	 * The fixtures are timing-aware: only the span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain()
5	 * and rt_phase_vsim_drain() may touch libgloss / sim_control MMIO. Outside
6	 * that window the program runs on QEMU virt, which has no peripheral at
7	 * 0xE0080000.
8	 */
9	
10	#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
11	#define RT_C_HELPERS_H
12	
13	#include <stdint.h>
14	#include <stdio.h>
15	
16	/* Matches src/platform/sim_control.hpp:32 SimCommand::FAILED. */
17	#define SIM_CONTROL_REG (*(volatile uint32_t*) 0xE0080000)
18	#define SIM_FAILED 0x01234569u
19	
20	/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
21	 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
22	 *
23	 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
24	 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
25	 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
26	 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
27	static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
28	{
29	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
30	                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
31	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
32	}
33	static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
34	{
35	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
36	                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
37	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
38	}
39	
40	/* Vsim-only failure path: writes SimCommand::FAILED so vsim's
41	 * handle_sim_command (sim_control.hpp:188) sets exits=1 and the simulator
42	 * loop returns 1 before the handoff fires. The harness sees RC=1 vs
43	 * EXPECT_RC=0 and reports a failure. Bypasses QEMU2 entirely. */
44	__attribute__((noreturn)) static inline void rt_fail(void)
45	{
46	  fflush(stdout);
47	  SIM_CONTROL_REG = SIM_FAILED;
48	  for (;;) {
49	  }
50	}
51	
52	/* RISC-V semihosting calls. Only safe in the QEMU2 phase (after
53	 * rt_phase_vsim_drain): QEMU is spawned with -semihosting-config
54	 * enable=on,target=native (qemu_handback.hpp:131-132) so the magic
55	 * slli/ebreak/srai triple is intercepted before reaching mtvec. vsim
56	 * does NOT implement semihosting, so executing these inside the vsim
57	 * span would route to m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loop. */
58	static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
59	{
60	  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
61	  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
62	  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
63	                       ".option norvc\n"
64	                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
65	                       "ebreak\n"
66	                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
67	                       ".option pop\n"
68	                       :
69	                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
70	                       : "memory");
71	}
72	
73	#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */
74

1	# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.

21	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests

28	                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
29	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
30	# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
31	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
32	                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

34	.PHONY: all clean dump
35

37	     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
38	     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
39	     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)
40	
41	spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
42		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
43		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@
44	
45	handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
46		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
47		$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@
48	
49	handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
50		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
51		$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@
52	
53	handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
54		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
55		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@
56	
57	$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
58		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
59		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
60	
61	# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
62	# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
63	# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
64	# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
65	# the .text wildcard.
66	$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
67		$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@
68	
69	# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
70	# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
71	$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
72		$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@
73	
74	# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
75	PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
76	$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
77		$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
78		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
79	
80	dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
81		$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
82		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
83		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf
84	
85	clean:

87		      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
88		      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
89		      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
90		      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`.

## Linking a Fixture That Runs on Both QEMU and vsim

The same ELF executes in QEMU 1, vsim, then QEMU 2. Each simulator advertises a different set of host-call peripherals, so the C runtime has to be split into pieces that work in the right phase. Source for everything below: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/`.

### Three flavors of fixture

| Tier         | Examples                                       | Linker script                | Toolchain flags                                     | Newlib? |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------|
| Pure asm     | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival` | `handoff_roundtrip.ld`        | `-march=rv64gc -nostdlib -static`                   | no      |
| C-level      | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_file_io`    | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gc -nostartfiles -static`               | yes     |
| C-level + V  | `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`                   | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gcv -nostartfiles -static`              | yes     |

C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c}` ahead of the `.c` file. Asm fixtures skip the runtime entirely and inline the semihosting-exit triple via `semihost_exit.inc`.

### Memory layout (`runtime/rt_c.ld`)

All fixtures load at `0x80000000` - QEMU virt's RAM base, also inside vsim's RAM region (`0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

```
0x80000000  .text         (entry _start; .text.init ordered first)
            .rodata
            .data
            _edata
            .bss          (zeroed by crt0)
            _end          (heap base used by _sbrk)
   ...
0x80700000  _stack        (sp initialized here in crt0; PROVIDE'd, override
                           with -Wl,--defsym,_stack=...)
0x80FF0000  V_SCRATCH     (kVScratchGpa - 2 KB reserved for V regfile transport)
0x88000000  end of QEMU virt's default 128 MB RAM
```

The 9 MB gap between `_stack` and `V_SCRATCH` keeps the V scratch region above any plausible stack use.

### `runtime/crt0.S` - the C entry point

Lands at `0x80000000` (`.text.init` orders first inside the wildcard match) and:

1. Enables icache + dcache via `mcache_ctl`.
2. Sets `sp` from the linker's `_stack`, `gp` from `__global_pointer$`.
3. **Sets `mstatus.FS=Initial` and `mstatus.VS=Initial`** so FP and V instructions don't trap. Critical for V fixtures: `mstatus.VS=Off` would make `vsetvli` raise illegal-insn the moment the binary's first V instruction runs in vsim.
4. Enables unaligned access via `mmisc_ctl.UNA` (Andes bit).
5. Parks any non-zero hart at a poll loop (single-hart fixtures).
6. Clears `.bss` between `_edata` and `_end`.
7. Calls `init_mtvec` (in `handler.c`) - registers `m_trap_entry` as the trap vector and seeds the syscall table.
8. `call main; call _exit`.

### `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` - the trap path

`m_trap_entry` (in `trap.S`) is a flat M-mode prologue that saves x1, x3..x31, `mepc`, `mstatus`, `mxstatus` onto the stack, then tail-calls `handle_trap(mcause, sp)`. `handle_trap` dispatches:

- `mcause == 0xb` (machine ECALL) -> `handle_syscall`, which routes through `syscall_table[a7]` to a libgloss stub, returns into `a0`, and bumps `mepc += 4`.
- Anything else -> `exception_handler`, which logs `mcause/mdcause/mepc/mtval` and calls `_exit(mcause)`.

The epilogue restores everything and `mret`s. No priv-mode change; the entire fixture runs in M-mode.

### `runtime/libgloss.c` - HTIF and semihosting side-by-side

Two host-call channels live in the same file because each only works on one of the two simulators:

| Newlib stub                   | Backend                                                              | Where it works |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| `_open`/`_read`/`_lseek`/`_fstat`/... and `_write(fd>2)` | HTIF magic-mem syscall via `frontend_syscall` -> `sim_syscall` -> `*TOHOST_CMD_REG` (`0xE0080010`) | **vsim only** |
| `_write(fd<=2)` (printf to stdout/stderr) | byte stream to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` (`0xE0080004`)                    | **vsim only** |
| `_exit(status)`               | RISC-V semihosting magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` with `a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED=0x20` | **QEMU only** (when launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) |

The asymmetry is load-bearing. QEMU virt has no peripheral at `0xE008xxxx`, so an HTIF write there raises a store-access-fault. vsim does not implement RISC-V semihosting, so the magic triple traps into `m_trap_entry -> exception_handler` and loops. **A single binary cannot make `printf` and `_exit` work in the same phase.** The runtime gets away with bundling them only because the drain marker (`csrwi 0x7C0, 1`) fires *before* `_exit` is reached: vsim sees the libgloss-backed stdio, then exits; QEMU 2 inherits the resumed state, never touches HTIF, runs to `_exit`, which trips semihosting.

`qemu_handback.hpp` always launches QEMU 2 with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` and `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` so the magic triple is intercepted and the Andes vendor CSRs touched by crt0 (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl) are recognised.

### Phase-aware MMIO contract (`runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`)

Because each MMIO endpoint is single-simulator, C fixtures have to know which phase they are in. `rt_c_helpers.h` makes the contract explicit:

```c
// QEMU 1 phase: pure register/memory work only. No printf, no fopen.
//   (HTIF would trap; semihosting magic would land in QEMU's stub but
//    is reserved for the QEMU 2 _exit path.)
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();   // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 0  + .globl _hybrid_enter_pc

// vsim phase: libgloss / sim_control / printf / fopen are SAFE here.
rt_phase_vsim_drain();    // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 1  + .globl _hybrid_exit_pc

// QEMU 2 phase: rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) and _exit (SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED)
//   are SAFE here. Returning from main() falls into _exit naturally.
```

The two `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels are inert in CSR-trigger mode; `run_e2e.sh` resolves them via `nm --defined-only` for PC-trigger mode and passes them as `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` to vsim and `enter_pc=0x...` to the QEMU plugin.

### Pure-asm fixtures (Tier 1) skip all of this

`handoff_*`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, etc. link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` (no `.bss`, no `_stack`, no global pointer) and inline the semihosting exit triple from `semihost_exit.inc`. No crt0, no newlib, no trap handler. They are used for register-survival tests where C overhead would obscure the signal under test.

## Where to Look Next

+The epilogue restores everything and `mret`s. No priv-mode change; the entire fixture runs in M-mode.
+### `runtime/libgloss.c` - HTIF and semihosting side-by-side
+Two host-call channels live in the same file because each only works on one of the two simulators:
+| Newlib stub                   | Backend                                                              | Where it works |
+| `_open`/`_read`/`_lseek`/`_fstat`/... and `_write(fd>2)` | HTIF magic-mem syscall via `frontend_syscall` -> `sim_syscall` -> `*TOHOST_CMD_REG` (`0xE0080010`) | **vsim only** |
+| `_write(fd<=2)` (printf to stdout/stderr) | byte stream to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` (`0xE0080004`)                    | **vsim only** |

+| `_exit(status)`               | RISC-V semihosting magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` with `a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED=0x20` | **QEMU only** (when launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) |

+The asymmetry is load-bearing. QEMU virt has no peripheral at `0xE008xxxx`, so an HTIF write there raises a store-access-fault. vsim does not implement RISC-V semihosting, so the magic triple traps into `m_trap_entry -> exception_handler` and loops. **A single binary cannot make `printf` and `_exit` work in the same phase.** The runtime gets away with bundling them only because the drain marker (`csrwi 0x7C0, 1`) fires *before* `_exit` is reached: vsim sees the libgloss-backed stdio, then exits; QEMU 2 inherits the resumed state, never touches HTIF, runs to `_exit`, which trips semihosting.

+`qemu_handback.hpp` always launches QEMU 2 with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` and `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` so the magic triple is intercepted and the Andes vendor CSRs touched by crt0 (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl) are recognised.
+### Phase-aware MMIO contract (`runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`)
+Because each MMIO endpoint is single-simulator, C fixtures have to know which phase they are in. `rt_c_helpers.h` makes the contract explicit:
+// QEMU 1 phase: pure register/memory work only. No printf, no fopen.
+//   (HTIF would trap; semihosting magic would land in QEMU's stub but
+//    is reserved for the QEMU 2 _exit path.)
+rt_phase_qemu1_drain();   // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 0  + .globl _hybrid_enter_pc
+// vsim phase: libgloss / sim_control / printf / fopen are SAFE here.
+rt_phase_vsim_drain();    // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 1  + .globl _hybrid_exit_pc
+// QEMU 2 phase: rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) and _exit (SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED)
+//   are SAFE here. Returning from main() falls into _exit naturally.

+The two `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels are inert in CSR-trigger mode; `run_e2e.sh` resolves them via `nm --defined-only` for PC-trigger mode and passes them as `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` to vsim and `enter_pc=0x...` to the QEMU plugin.
+### Pure-asm fixtures (Tier 1) skip all of this

+`handoff_*`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, etc. link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` (no `.bss`, no `_stack`, no global pointer) and inline the semihosting exit triple from `semihost_exit.inc`. No crt0, no newlib, no trap handler. They are used for register-survival tests where C overhead would obscure the signal under test.
## Where to Look Next
- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract.
