## Linking a Fixture That Runs on Both QEMU and vsim

The same ELF executes in QEMU 1, vsim, then QEMU 2. Each simulator advertises a different set of host-call peripherals, so the C runtime has to be split into pieces that work in the right phase. Source for everything below: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/`.

### Three flavors of fixture

| Tier         | Examples                                       | Linker script                | Toolchain flags                                     | Newlib? |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------|
| Pure asm     | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival` | `handoff_roundtrip.ld`        | `-march=rv64gc -nostdlib -static`                   | no      |
| C-level      | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_file_io`    | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gc -nostartfiles -static`               | yes     |
| C-level + V  | `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`                   | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gcv -nostartfiles -static`              | yes     |

C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c}` ahead of the `.c` file. Asm fixtures skip the runtime entirely and inline the semihosting-exit triple via `semihost_exit.inc`.

### Memory layout (`runtime/rt_c.ld`)

All fixtures load at `0x80000000` - QEMU virt's RAM base, also inside vsim's RAM region (`0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

```
0x80000000  .text         (entry _start; .text.init ordered first)
            .rodata
            .data
            _edata
            .bss          (zeroed by crt0)
            _end          (heap base used by _sbrk)
   ...
0x80700000  _stack        (sp initialized here in crt0; PROVIDE'd, override
                           with -Wl,--defsym,_stack=...)
0x80FF0000  V_SCRATCH     (kVScratchGpa - 2 KB reserved for V regfile transport)
0x88000000  end of QEMU virt's default 128 MB RAM
```

The 9 MB gap between `_stack` and `V_SCRATCH` keeps the V scratch region above any plausible stack use.

### `runtime/crt0.S` - the C entry point

Lands at `0x80000000` (`.text.init` orders first inside the wildcard match) and:

1. Enables icache + dcache via `mcache_ctl`.
2. Sets `sp` from the linker's `_stack`, `gp` from `__global_pointer$`.
3. **Sets `mstatus.FS=Initial` and `mstatus.VS=Initial`** so FP and V instructions don't trap. Critical for V fixtures: `mstatus.VS=Off` would make `vsetvli` raise illegal-insn the moment the binary's first V instruction runs in vsim.
4. Enables unaligned access via `mmisc_ctl.UNA` (Andes bit).
5. Parks any non-zero hart at a poll loop (single-hart fixtures).
6. Clears `.bss` between `_edata` and `_end`.
7. Calls `init_mtvec` (in `handler.c`) - registers `m_trap_entry` as the trap vector and seeds the syscall table.
8. `call main; call _exit`.

### `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` - the trap path

`m_trap_entry` (in `trap.S`) is a flat M-mode prologue that saves x1, x3..x31, `mepc`, `mstatus`, `mxstatus` onto the stack, then tail-calls `handle_trap(mcause, sp)`. `handle_trap` dispatches:

- `mcause == 0xb` (machine ECALL) -> `handle_syscall`, which routes through `syscall_table[a7]` to a libgloss stub, returns into `a0`, and bumps `mepc += 4`.
- Anything else -> `exception_handler`, which logs `mcause/mdcause/mepc/mtval` and calls `_exit(mcause)`.

The epilogue restores everything and `mret`s. No priv-mode change; the entire fixture runs in M-mode.

### `runtime/libgloss.c` - HTIF and semihosting side-by-side

Two host-call channels live in the same file because each only works on one of the two simulators:

| Newlib stub                   | Backend                                                              | Where it works |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| `_open`/`_read`/`_lseek`/`_fstat`/... and `_write(fd>2)` | HTIF magic-mem syscall via `frontend_syscall` -> `sim_syscall` -> `*TOHOST_CMD_REG` (`0xE0080010`) | **vsim only** |
| `_write(fd<=2)` (printf to stdout/stderr) | byte stream to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` (`0xE0080004`)                    | **vsim only** |
| `_exit(status)`               | RISC-V semihosting magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` with `a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED=0x20` | **QEMU only** (when launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) |

The asymmetry is load-bearing. QEMU virt has no peripheral at `0xE008xxxx`, so an HTIF write there raises a store-access-fault. vsim does not implement RISC-V semihosting, so the magic triple traps into `m_trap_entry -> exception_handler` and loops. **A single binary cannot make `printf` and `_exit` work in the same phase.** The runtime gets away with bundling them only because the drain marker (`csrwi 0x7C0, 1`) fires *before* `_exit` is reached: vsim sees the libgloss-backed stdio, then exits; QEMU 2 inherits the resumed state, never touches HTIF, runs to `_exit`, which trips semihosting.

`qemu_handback.hpp` always launches QEMU 2 with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` and `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` so the magic triple is intercepted and the Andes vendor CSRs touched by crt0 (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl) are recognised.

### Phase-aware MMIO contract (`runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`)

Because each MMIO endpoint is single-simulator, C fixtures have to know which phase they are in. `rt_c_helpers.h` makes the contract explicit:

```c
// QEMU 1 phase: pure register/memory work only. No printf, no fopen.
//   (HTIF would trap; semihosting magic would land in QEMU's stub but
//    is reserved for the QEMU 2 _exit path.)
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();   // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 0  + .globl _hybrid_enter_pc

// vsim phase: libgloss / sim_control / printf / fopen are SAFE here.
rt_phase_vsim_drain();    // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 1  + .globl _hybrid_exit_pc

// QEMU 2 phase: rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) and _exit (SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED)
//   are SAFE here. Returning from main() falls into _exit naturally.
```

The two `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels are inert in CSR-trigger mode; `run_e2e.sh` resolves them via `nm --defined-only` for PC-trigger mode and passes them as `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` to vsim and `enter_pc=0x...` to the QEMU plugin.

### Pure-asm fixtures (Tier 1) skip all of this

`handoff_*`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, etc. link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` (no `.bss`, no `_stack`, no global pointer) and inline the semihosting exit triple from `semihost_exit.inc`. No crt0, no newlib, no trap handler. They are used for register-survival tests where C overhead would obscure the signal under test.

## Where to Look Next
toolUseResult.newString:
## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For

You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists.

### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase

| Phase   | HTIF (`0xE008_xxxx`)                          | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak                                   | What you can do                                              |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| QEMU 1  | unmapped -> store-access-fault, infinite trap | not enabled at QEMU 1 launch                                       | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no `fopen`, no `_exit`. |
| vsim    | mapped (sim_control / display)                 | not implemented -> trap to `m_trap_entry` -> infinite trap        | `printf`, `fopen`, full newlib via libgloss-HTIF             |
| QEMU 2  | unmapped -> store-access-fault                 | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)            | semihosting (`_exit`, `SYS_WRITE0`, etc). **No `printf`/`fopen`**. |

The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) makes the contract explicit:

```c
// ===== QEMU 1 window =====        // pure regs/memory only
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();             // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  -> hand off to vsim
// ===== vsim window =====          // printf, fopen, libgloss MMIO are OK
rt_phase_vsim_drain();              // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  -> hand off to QEMU 2
// ===== QEMU 2 window =====        // semihosting OK; libgloss MMIO is NOT
```

If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.**

### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation

vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely:

- Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost.
- Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost.
- Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost.
- Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins).

If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C.

### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates

`v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes.

`rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example.

### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains

ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.

### 5. Don't run V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain

The TCG plugin reads PC at retire of `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`; when a V insn is in flight immediately beforehand, the readback misreports the drain PC and vsim resumes at the wrong instruction. Pattern: keep Phase A scalar (seed memory with `sw`/`sd`, set up GPRs); do all V work inside the vsim window. `rt_c_v_regs.c` calls this out in its header comment.

### 6. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`

The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it.

### 7. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window

Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts:

- `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`).
- `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`).
- `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code).
- Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`.

Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim.

### 8. Microarchitectural state does not transfer

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.

### Where each rule lives in the runtime

| Concern                                         | File                                           |
|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address)      | `runtime/rt_c.ld`                              |
| `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear      | `runtime/crt0.S`                               |
| M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch         | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
| HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`)       |
| Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit`                    |
| Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...)      | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |
| QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` |

For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

## Where to Look Next

+If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.**
-0x80000000  .text         (entry _start; .text.init ordered first)
-            .rodata
-            .data
-            _edata
-            .bss          (zeroed by crt0)
-            _end          (heap base used by _sbrk)
-0x80700000  _stack        (sp initialized here in crt0; PROVIDE'd, override
-                           with -Wl,--defsym,_stack=...)
-0x80FF0000  V_SCRATCH     (kVScratchGpa - 2 KB reserved for V regfile transport)
-0x88000000  end of QEMU virt's default 128 MB RAM

-The 9 MB gap between `_stack` and `V_SCRATCH` keeps the V scratch region above any plausible stack use.

+vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely:
-### `runtime/crt0.S` - the C entry point
+- Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost.
+- Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost.
+- Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost.
+- Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins).
-Lands at `0x80000000` (`.text.init` orders first inside the wildcard match) and:

+If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C.
-1. Enables icache + dcache via `mcache_ctl`.
-2. Sets `sp` from the linker's `_stack`, `gp` from `__global_pointer$`.

-3. **Sets `mstatus.FS=Initial` and `mstatus.VS=Initial`** so FP and V instructions don't trap. Critical for V fixtures: `mstatus.VS=Off` would make `vsetvli` raise illegal-insn the moment the binary's first V instruction runs in vsim.
-4. Enables unaligned access via `mmisc_ctl.UNA` (Andes bit).
-5. Parks any non-zero hart at a poll loop (single-hart fixtures).
-6. Clears `.bss` between `_edata` and `_end`.
-7. Calls `init_mtvec` (in `handler.c`) - registers `m_trap_entry` as the trap vector and seeds the syscall table.
-8. `call main; call _exit`.
+### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates
-### `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` - the trap path

+`v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes.
-`m_trap_entry` (in `trap.S`) is a flat M-mode prologue that saves x1, x3..x31, `mepc`, `mstatus`, `mxstatus` onto the stack, then tail-calls `handle_trap(mcause, sp)`. `handle_trap` dispatches:
+`rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example.
-- `mcause == 0xb` (machine ECALL) -> `handle_syscall`, which routes through `syscall_table[a7]` to a libgloss stub, returns into `a0`, and bumps `mepc += 4`.
-- Anything else -> `exception_handler`, which logs `mcause/mdcause/mepc/mtval` and calls `_exit(mcause)`.
+### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains
-The epilogue restores everything and `mret`s. No priv-mode change; the entire fixture runs in M-mode.

+ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
-### `runtime/libgloss.c` - HTIF and semihosting side-by-side
+### 5. Don't run V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain
-Two host-call channels live in the same file because each only works on one of the two simulators:

+The TCG plugin reads PC at retire of `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`; when a V insn is in flight immediately beforehand, the readback misreports the drain PC and vsim resumes at the wrong instruction. Pattern: keep Phase A scalar (seed memory with `sw`/`sd`, set up GPRs); do all V work inside the vsim window. `rt_c_v_regs.c` calls this out in its header comment.
-| Newlib stub                   | Backend                                                              | Where it works |
-| `_open`/`_read`/`_lseek`/`_fstat`/... and `_write(fd>2)` | HTIF magic-mem syscall via `frontend_syscall` -> `sim_syscall` -> `*TOHOST_CMD_REG` (`0xE0080010`) | **vsim only** |
-| `_write(fd<=2)` (printf to stdout/stderr) | byte stream to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` (`0xE0080004`)                    | **vsim only** |

-| `_exit(status)`               | RISC-V semihosting magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` with `a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED=0x20` | **QEMU only** (when launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) |
+### 6. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`

-The asymmetry is load-bearing. QEMU virt has no peripheral at `0xE008xxxx`, so an HTIF write there raises a store-access-fault. vsim does not implement RISC-V semihosting, so the magic triple traps into `m_trap_entry -> exception_handler` and loops. **A single binary cannot make `printf` and `_exit` work in the same phase.** The runtime gets away with bundling them only because the drain marker (`csrwi 0x7C0, 1`) fires *before* `_exit` is reached: vsim sees the libgloss-backed stdio, then exits; QEMU 2 inherits the resumed state, never touches HTIF, runs to `_exit`, which trips semihosting.

+The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it.

-`qemu_handback.hpp` always launches QEMU 2 with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` and `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` so the magic triple is intercepted and the Andes vendor CSRs touched by crt0 (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl) are recognised.
+### 7. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window
-### Phase-aware MMIO contract (`runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`)
+Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts:
-Because each MMIO endpoint is single-simulator, C fixtures have to know which phase they are in. `rt_c_helpers.h` makes the contract explicit:
+- `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`).
+- `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`).
+- `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code).
+- Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`.
-// QEMU 1 phase: pure register/memory work only. No printf, no fopen.
-//   (HTIF would trap; semihosting magic would land in QEMU's stub but
-//    is reserved for the QEMU 2 _exit path.)
-rt_phase_qemu1_drain();   // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 0  + .globl _hybrid_enter_pc

+Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim.
-// vsim phase: libgloss / sim_control / printf / fopen are SAFE here.
-rt_phase_vsim_drain();    // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 1  + .globl _hybrid_exit_pc
+### 8. Microarchitectural state does not transfer
-// QEMU 2 phase: rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) and _exit (SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED)
-//   are SAFE here. Returning from main() falls into _exit naturally.

+Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.

-The two `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels are inert in CSR-trigger mode; `run_e2e.sh` resolves them via `nm --defined-only` for PC-trigger mode and passes them as `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` to vsim and `enter_pc=0x...` to the QEMU plugin.
+### Where each rule lives in the runtime
-### Pure-asm fixtures (Tier 1) skip all of this
+| Concern                                         | File                                           |
+| Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address)      | `runtime/rt_c.ld`                              |
+| `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear      | `runtime/crt0.S`                               |
+| M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch         | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
+| HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`)       |
+| Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit`                    |
+| Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...)      | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
+| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |
+| QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` |

-`handoff_*`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, etc. link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` (no `.bss`, no `_stack`, no global pointer) and inline the semihosting exit triple from `semihost_exit.inc`. No crt0, no newlib, no trap handler. They are used for register-survival tests where C overhead would obscure the signal under test.

+For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

## Linking a Fixture That Runs on Both QEMU and vsim

The same ELF executes in QEMU 1, vsim, then QEMU 2. Each simulator advertises a different set of host-call peripherals, so the C runtime has to be split into pieces that work in the right phase. Source for everything below: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/`.

### Three flavors of fixture

| Tier         | Examples                                       | Linker script                | Toolchain flags                                     | Newlib? |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------|
| Pure asm     | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival` | `handoff_roundtrip.ld`        | `-march=rv64gc -nostdlib -static`                   | no      |
| C-level      | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_file_io`    | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gc -nostartfiles -static`               | yes     |
| C-level + V  | `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`                   | `runtime/rt_c.ld`            | `-march=rv64gcv -nostartfiles -static`              | yes     |

C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c}` ahead of the `.c` file. Asm fixtures skip the runtime entirely and inline the semihosting-exit triple via `semihost_exit.inc`.

### Memory layout (`runtime/rt_c.ld`)

All fixtures load at `0x80000000` - QEMU virt's RAM base, also inside vsim's RAM region (`0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

```
0x80000000  .text         (entry _start; .text.init ordered first)
            .rodata
            .data
            _edata
            .bss          (zeroed by crt0)
            _end          (heap base used by _sbrk)
   ...
0x80700000  _stack        (sp initialized here in crt0; PROVIDE'd, override
                           with -Wl,--defsym,_stack=...)
0x80FF0000  V_SCRATCH     (kVScratchGpa - 2 KB reserved for V regfile transport)
0x88000000  end of QEMU virt's default 128 MB RAM
```

The 9 MB gap between `_stack` and `V_SCRATCH` keeps the V scratch region above any plausible stack use.

### `runtime/crt0.S` - the C entry point

Lands at `0x80000000` (`.text.init` orders first inside the wildcard match) and:

1. Enables icache + dcache via `mcache_ctl`.
2. Sets `sp` from the linker's `_stack`, `gp` from `__global_pointer$`.
3. **Sets `mstatus.FS=Initial` and `mstatus.VS=Initial`** so FP and V instructions don't trap. Critical for V fixtures: `mstatus.VS=Off` would make `vsetvli` raise illegal-insn the moment the binary's first V instruction runs in vsim.
4. Enables unaligned access via `mmisc_ctl.UNA` (Andes bit).
5. Parks any non-zero hart at a poll loop (single-hart fixtures).
6. Clears `.bss` between `_edata` and `_end`.
7. Calls `init_mtvec` (in `handler.c`) - registers `m_trap_entry` as the trap vector and seeds the syscall table.
8. `call main; call _exit`.

### `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` - the trap path

`m_trap_entry` (in `trap.S`) is a flat M-mode prologue that saves x1, x3..x31, `mepc`, `mstatus`, `mxstatus` onto the stack, then tail-calls `handle_trap(mcause, sp)`. `handle_trap` dispatches:

- `mcause == 0xb` (machine ECALL) -> `handle_syscall`, which routes through `syscall_table[a7]` to a libgloss stub, returns into `a0`, and bumps `mepc += 4`.
- Anything else -> `exception_handler`, which logs `mcause/mdcause/mepc/mtval` and calls `_exit(mcause)`.

The epilogue restores everything and `mret`s. No priv-mode change; the entire fixture runs in M-mode.

### `runtime/libgloss.c` - HTIF and semihosting side-by-side

Two host-call channels live in the same file because each only works on one of the two simulators:

| Newlib stub                   | Backend                                                              | Where it works |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| `_open`/`_read`/`_lseek`/`_fstat`/... and `_write(fd>2)` | HTIF magic-mem syscall via `frontend_syscall` -> `sim_syscall` -> `*TOHOST_CMD_REG` (`0xE0080010`) | **vsim only** |
| `_write(fd<=2)` (printf to stdout/stderr) | byte stream to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR` (`0xE0080004`)                    | **vsim only** |
| `_exit(status)`               | RISC-V semihosting magic triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` with `a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED=0x20` | **QEMU only** (when launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) |

The asymmetry is load-bearing. QEMU virt has no peripheral at `0xE008xxxx`, so an HTIF write there raises a store-access-fault. vsim does not implement RISC-V semihosting, so the magic triple traps into `m_trap_entry -> exception_handler` and loops. **A single binary cannot make `printf` and `_exit` work in the same phase.** The runtime gets away with bundling them only because the drain marker (`csrwi 0x7C0, 1`) fires *before* `_exit` is reached: vsim sees the libgloss-backed stdio, then exits; QEMU 2 inherits the resumed state, never touches HTIF, runs to `_exit`, which trips semihosting.

`qemu_handback.hpp` always launches QEMU 2 with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` and `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` so the magic triple is intercepted and the Andes vendor CSRs touched by crt0 (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl) are recognised.

### Phase-aware MMIO contract (`runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`)

Because each MMIO endpoint is single-simulator, C fixtures have to know which phase they are in. `rt_c_helpers.h` makes the contract explicit:

```c
// QEMU 1 phase: pure register/memory work only. No printf, no fopen.
//   (HTIF would trap; semihosting magic would land in QEMU's stub but
//    is reserved for the QEMU 2 _exit path.)
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();   // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 0  + .globl _hybrid_enter_pc

// vsim phase: libgloss / sim_control / printf / fopen are SAFE here.
rt_phase_vsim_drain();    // emits csrwi 0x7C0, 1  + .globl _hybrid_exit_pc

// QEMU 2 phase: rt_semihost_writes (SYS_WRITE0) and _exit (SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED)
//   are SAFE here. Returning from main() falls into _exit naturally.
```

The two `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels are inert in CSR-trigger mode; `run_e2e.sh` resolves them via `nm --defined-only` for PC-trigger mode and passes them as `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` to vsim and `enter_pc=0x...` to the QEMU plugin.

### Pure-asm fixtures (Tier 1) skip all of this

`handoff_*`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, etc. link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` (no `.bss`, no `_stack`, no global pointer) and inline the semihosting exit triple from `semihost_exit.inc`. No crt0, no newlib, no trap handler. They are used for register-survival tests where C overhead would obscure the signal under test.

## Where to Look Next

## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For

You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists.

### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase

| Phase   | HTIF (`0xE008_xxxx`)                          | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak                                   | What you can do                                              |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| QEMU 1  | unmapped -> store-access-fault, infinite trap | not enabled at QEMU 1 launch                                       | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no `fopen`, no `_exit`. |
| vsim    | mapped (sim_control / display)                 | not implemented -> trap to `m_trap_entry` -> infinite trap        | `printf`, `fopen`, full newlib via libgloss-HTIF             |
| QEMU 2  | unmapped -> store-access-fault                 | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)            | semihosting (`_exit`, `SYS_WRITE0`, etc). **No `printf`/`fopen`**. |

The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) makes the contract explicit:

```c
// ===== QEMU 1 window =====        // pure regs/memory only
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();             // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  -> hand off to vsim
// ===== vsim window =====          // printf, fopen, libgloss MMIO are OK
rt_phase_vsim_drain();              // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  -> hand off to QEMU 2
// ===== QEMU 2 window =====        // semihosting OK; libgloss MMIO is NOT
```

If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.**

### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation

vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely:

- Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost.
- Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost.
- Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost.
- Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins).

If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C.

### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates

`v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes.

`rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example.

### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains

ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.

### 5. Don't run V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain

The TCG plugin reads PC at retire of `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`; when a V insn is in flight immediately beforehand, the readback misreports the drain PC and vsim resumes at the wrong instruction. Pattern: keep Phase A scalar (seed memory with `sw`/`sd`, set up GPRs); do all V work inside the vsim window. `rt_c_v_regs.c` calls this out in its header comment.

### 6. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`

The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it.

### 7. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window

Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts:

- `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`).
- `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`).
- `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code).
- Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`.

Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim.

### 8. Microarchitectural state does not transfer

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.

### Where each rule lives in the runtime

| Concern                                         | File                                           |
|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address)      | `runtime/rt_c.ld`                              |
| `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear      | `runtime/crt0.S`                               |
| M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch         | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
| HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`)       |
| Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit`                    |
| Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...)      | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |
| QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` |

For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

## Where to Look Next

Replaced the prior tour-of-files section with a gotcha-first answer to "what should I take care of when one program runs across both simulators." The eight constraints, with the *why* attached:

1. **MMIO addresses mean different things per phase** - HTIF works only in vsim, semihosting works only in QEMU 2, QEMU 1 accepts neither. `printf` outside the vsim window traps; `_exit` outside QEMU 2 traps.
2. **Only GPRs/PC/V-state survive vsim mutation** - mutating `f3`, `mscratch`, PMP, etc. inside vsim is silently lost (passthrough overrides).
3. **V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates** - must `vle8.v`/`vse8.v` against `V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`.
4. **`mstatus.VS`/`.FS` must be set before the QEMU 1 drain** - ResumeDriver only writes mstatus when VS bits are already non-zero in the snapshot.
5. **No V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain** - plugin's PC readback misreports.
6. **Trigger insn must be 4-byte `csrwi`** - no compressed form.
7. **Stay inside the shared `0x80000000..0x88000000` window** - includes `_stack` at `0x80700000` and the off-limits `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000`.
8. **Microarch state does not transfer** - lr/sc reservations, caches, mcycle/minstret continuity all dropped.

Then a "where each rule lives" table mapping concern -> file (`rt_c.ld`, `crt0.S`, `trap.S`, `handler.c`, `libgloss.c`, `rt_c_helpers.h`, `qemu_handback.hpp`) so the reader can follow up. Pure-asm fixtures get one closing line: rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply.

Your question was: **"A linked program, part of code runs on QEMU and the other on vsim. What should I know to take care of?"**

You wanted the practical gotchas a programmer faces when authoring a single ELF that straddles QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2 - not a tour of `crt0.S`/`libgloss.c`/etc., which is what my first attempt gave you.

message.content: what is MMIO? explain " The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase" I don't understand at all

content.text:
**MMIO = Memory-Mapped I/O.** A way to talk to hardware (or to the simulator's host) by reading/writing specific RAM-looking addresses. There's no special "print" CPU instruction - instead, you do a normal store to a magic address, and the simulator notices and acts on it.

Concrete example - this is what `printf("A")` ultimately compiles to inside our libgloss:

```asm
li   t0, 0xE0080004   # the "console" address
li   a0, 'A'
sb   a0, 0(t0)        # store byte 'A' to that address
```

A normal `sb` (store-byte) instruction. To the CPU it looks identical to writing to RAM. The interesting thing is who is *watching* that address.

**vsim has a fake console wired to `0xE0080004`.** When the RTL store reaches the bus, vsim's `sim_control` peripheral catches it and forwards the byte to the host's stdout. So `sb` at `0xE0080004` prints `A`.

**QEMU virt has nothing at `0xE0080004`.** It's not mapped to RAM, not mapped to a peripheral, not mapped to anything. The store raises a *store-access-fault*, which jumps to `mtvec` (your trap handler), which prints "Trap occurred!" and dies in an infinite re-trap loop.

**Same instruction, same address, two different outcomes.** That's what "the same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase" means.

The flip side, semihosting, is the same idea backwards:

```asm
slli x0, x0, 0x1f    # magic 3-instruction sequence -
ebreak               #   QEMU's semihosting layer recognises this
srai x0, x0, 0x7     #   pattern and treats it as a syscall
```

QEMU 2 was launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, so QEMU intercepts the `ebreak` before the trap even fires and runs the syscall (e.g. `_exit(0)`).

vsim has no semihosting layer. The same `ebreak` just traps to `mtvec` -> trap handler -> infinite re-trap.

So:

| Address / pattern              | What watches it in **vsim**          | What watches it in **QEMU**          |
|--------------------------------|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `0xE0080004` (libgloss console) | sim_control peripheral - prints byte | nothing - store-access-fault         |
| `0xE0080010` (HTIF syscalls)    | sim_control - dispatches `_open`/`_read`/... | nothing - fault                      |
| `slli/ebreak/srai` triple       | nothing - normal `ebreak` -> trap    | semihosting layer - runs syscall     |

Each simulator implements *one* of the two host-call mechanisms, and they are not the same one. So your fixture has to use libgloss (the first two rows) only while vsim is running, and semihosting (the third row) only while QEMU 2 is running. QEMU 1 supports neither - it's just the boot phase, no I/O.

That's the whole trap (pun intended) behind rule #1.

content.text:
Concrete answer with your 3-printf example:

```c
int main(void) {
    printf("A\n");                  // Phase 1 (QEMU 1): TRAPS, infinite loop
    rt_phase_qemu1_drain();         // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  ->  hand off to vsim
    printf("B\n");                  // Phase 2 (vsim):  PRINTS
    rt_phase_vsim_drain();          // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  ->  hand off to QEMU 2
    printf("C\n");                  // Phase 3 (QEMU 2): TRAPS, infinite loop
    return 0;
}
```

**Why each line behaves differently when the source is identical:**

`printf("X")` always compiles to the same RISC-V instructions: store byte 'X' to address `0xE0080004`. The instructions don't change between phases. What changes is **who is listening at that address**:

- **QEMU 1**: nothing is mapped at `0xE0080004` -> store-access-fault -> trap handler -> re-fault forever. Crashes.
- **vsim**: the RTL has a fake "console" peripheral wired to `0xE0080004` -> the byte goes to your terminal. Prints.
- **QEMU 2**: same as QEMU 1, nothing mapped there. Crashes.

QEMU 2 *can* print, but only through a different mechanism - RISC-V semihosting (a magic `ebreak` pattern QEMU intercepts). QEMU 2 was launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on`, so:

```c
rt_semihost_writes("C\n");          // Phase 3: PRINTS
```

works. But that same call would trap in vsim (vsim doesn't implement semihosting) and trap in QEMU 1 (not enabled there either).

**Working version of your example:**

```c
int main(void) {
    // Phase 1: no print channel wired up; just do register/memory work.
    rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

    printf("B\n");                  // vsim:    libgloss -> 0xE0080004
    rt_phase_vsim_drain();

    rt_semihost_writes("C\n");      // QEMU 2:  semihosting ebreak
    return 0;
}
```

---

**What the fixture author has to take care of - the entire list:**

1. **Pick the right print function per phase**: `printf` only inside the vsim window; `rt_semihost_writes` only after the vsim drain (QEMU 2); nothing in QEMU 1.
2. **Always mark the phase boundaries** with `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()` and `rt_phase_vsim_drain()`. They are the magic CSR writes that hand control between simulators.
3. **Anything you mutate inside the vsim window only survives to QEMU 2 if it's a GPR, the PC, V state, or memory.** Mutating `f3`, `mscratch`, PMP, etc. is silently lost (snapshot from QEMU 1 wins).
4. **`mstatus.VS=Initial` and `.FS=Initial` must already be set in QEMU 1** before the drain. The standard `crt0.S` does this; if you skip it, set them yourself.
5. **No vector instructions in QEMU 1 before the drain marker** (the plugin's PC readback misreports if V is in flight).
6. **Stay inside the shared RAM window `0x80000000..0x88000000`**, and don't touch `0x80FF0000` (reserved for V regfile transport).

Rules 1 and 2 are the ones that bite first, because they make a "trivial" `printf` do completely different things in different parts of the same program.

content.text:
Direct answers to all three:

### Q3 — Are `f3`, `mscratch`, PMP restored in QEMU 2?

**Yes, they are restored** - just with the wrong value. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` sends gdbstub `P` packets for all 32 FPRs, 13 M-mode CSRs, and all 80 PMP slots into QEMU 2. So those registers are written.

The catch: the *value* sent to QEMU 2 is taken from the **QEMU 1 snapshot**, not from a fresh DM read of vsim's hart. vsim's `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` only re-reads x1..x31, PC, and V CSRs; everything else just flows through the snapshot unchanged.

So if your code does `fadd f3, ...` inside vsim, that mutation is silently overwritten by QEMU 1's old `f3` when QEMU 2 starts. From the program's perspective: **as if vsim never touched it.** That's what I meant by "lost."

If you need FP/M-CSR/PMP mutations to survive vsim, the fix has to land in vsim - extend `drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read those fields via DM Abstract Commands, the same way V CSRs are already handled.

### Q1 — Could QEMU 1 enable semihosting too?

Yes, two changes:

1. Add `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` to the QEMU 1 launch line in your shell driver (`rt_shared_mem.sh`, `roundtrip_e2e.sh`, etc).
2. In Phase 1 of the fixture, use `rt_semihost_writes("A\n")` instead of `printf` (libgloss `printf` would still trap because `0xE0080004` is unmapped in QEMU virt - this is unrelated to semihosting).

Caveat: don't fall into `_exit` (also a semihosting call) before the drain marker - that ends QEMU 1 cleanly *without* triggering the round-trip. Stick to `rt_semihost_writes` (`SYS_WRITE0`) for printing, leave `_exit` for the QEMU 2 phase.

### Q5 — Why no V instructions in QEMU 1, and how to enable them

**Root cause.** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c::on_handoff` reads the drain PC like this:

```c
v->pc = find_reg(regs, "pc");   // resolve_handles
...
s.pc = read_reg64(v->pc);       // on_handoff (drain time)
```

`qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")` calls into QEMU's gdbstub view of the architectural PC. With a V instruction retiring just before the `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`, that view can lag (QEMU's V helpers update `env->pc` at slightly different points than scalar insns), so the recorded `s.pc` does not point at the next instruction. vsim then resumes at the wrong PC and crashes or runs garbage.

**Proper fix (~5 lines in the plugin).** Don't read `pc` from the register file - compute it from the trigger insn's vaddr, which the plugin already has at translate time:

```c
// In vcpu_tb_trans, when registering the entry callback:
uintptr_t arg = ((uintptr_t)kind) | ((uintptr_t)qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) << 8);
qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb(insn, on_handoff,
                                       QEMU_PLUGIN_CB_R_REGS, (void*)arg);

// In on_handoff:
hybrid_handoff_kind kind = (uintptr_t)udata & 0xff;
uint64_t trigger_vaddr   = (uintptr_t)udata >> 8;
...
s.pc = trigger_vaddr + 4;   // csrwi is always 4-byte
```

After this, V instructions in Phase 1 are fine - the V state will be drained correctly by `read_reg_bytes(v->vreg[i], ...)` (already implemented), and the resume PC is no longer subject to the gdbstub-PC quirk.

**Workaround if you don't want to patch the plugin yet.** Insert a scalar settle sequence between the last V insn and the marker:

```asm
vse32.v   v0, (a0)
fence     rw, rw          # drain V pipeline
nop                       # let env->pc catch up
nop
csrwi     0x7C0, 0
```

Brittle and order-of-magnitude less safe than the plugin patch - I'd recommend the patch.

Want me to make the plugin change?

message.content:
1. what do "f3, mscratch, PMP" do? 
update into "limitation" in the document.

content.text:
Brief register tour first:

- **`f3`** - one of 32 floating-point registers (`f0..f31`). Holds an IEEE 754 single/double value. Touched by any `fadd`/`fmul`/`fld`/`fsd` etc.
- **`mscratch`** - a machine-mode CSR (0x340) used as scratch by the M-mode trap handler. Convention: holds a pointer to the per-hart context-save block, so the very first instruction of the trap entry can swap it with `sp` and start saving registers.
- **PMP** - Physical Memory Protection. A set of CSRs (`pmpcfg0..15`, `pmpaddr0..63`, in our struct as 16+64 entries) that define R/W/X permissions per address range for U/S-mode accesses. M-mode uses them to firewall lower-priv code out of M-only regions.

In the hybrid sim these are all "passthrough" - QEMU 1's snapshot wins, vsim mutations are silently dropped.

Now adding a `Known Limitations` section to the doc.

input.new_string:
For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

## Known Limitations

These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list.

### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped

**What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind.

**Affected registers:**
- All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`).
- 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped.
- All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`).
- `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target).

**Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP.

**Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase.

**Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block.

### L2. No V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain marker

**What this means.** Phase 1 must stay scalar. Setting up V regfile contents or V-CSRs in QEMU 1 is fine in principle (the plugin drains all of them), but if the *last instruction before* `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` is a V instruction, vsim resumes at the wrong PC.

**Why.** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c::on_handoff` reads the drain PC by calling `qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")`. QEMU's gdbstub-backed view of `env->pc` can lag for V helper-implemented instructions, so the recorded `s.pc` does not point at the instruction immediately after the marker.

**Workaround.** Either (a) insert a `fence rw, rw` plus a couple of `nop`s between the last V insn and the marker, or (b) seed V state in Phase 1 only via memory (write to addresses that Phase 2 will `vle*.v` from), keeping all V instructions inside the vsim window.

**Permanent fix.** Compute the drain PC from `qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) + 4` (`csrwi` is always 4-byte) at translate time and stash it in the per-callback userdata. Five-line change in the plugin.

### L3. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording.

### L4. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs

The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug.

### L5. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined

Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone.

### L6. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported

The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it.

### L7. Single hart only (today)

The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end.

## Where to Look Next

content.text:
Added a `Known Limitations` section with seven entries:

- **L1**: FP/M-CSR/PMP mutations in vsim are dropped; lists the affected registers and why; gives memory-routing workaround and the `state_drain.hpp` fix path.
- **L2**: V in QEMU 1 before the drain marker; explains the `qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")` lag; gives both fixture-side workaround and the 5-line plugin patch.
- **L3**: Microarch state dropped (caches/TLB/BPU/lr-sc reservations).
- **L4**: `mcycle`/`minstret` discontinuity.
- **L5**: MMIO inside the ROI undefined (HTIF is the only address that works in vsim).
- **L6**: SMC across the trigger undefined.
- **L7**: Single-hart only today.

Each entry has the *what*, *why*, *workaround*, and (where it exists) *permanent fix path*.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **Initial request**: Update `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` to describe how to exchange all CPU states (including vector registers), with design rationale and an example combining vector register + memory case. Focus on core architecture, no details.
   - **Second request**: Update the doc to reflect the **latest** architecture (catch implementation drift).
   - **Third request**: Add a section describing how linking handles parts running on QEMU vs vsim - specifically asked about libgloss, crt0, trap handler, semihosting.
   - **User clarified intent (4th)**: They wanted the practical "what gotchas should a fixture author take care of" - NOT a tour of runtime files. Said "You didn't answer my question."
   - **MMIO clarification**: User did not understand "the same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase"; needed explanation from scratch.
   - **Concrete example request**: User asked: "3 prints, one for QEMU 1, one for vsim, one for QEMU 2. how do they handle the printf?"
   - **Three follow-up questions**: 
     - Q3: Confirm whether f3, mscratch, PMP are not restored in QEMU 2.
     - Q1: Could QEMU 1 enable semihosting too?
     - Q5: Why no vector instructions in QEMU 1? "I need to support this."
   - **Final request**: Explain what f3/mscratch/PMP do, and update limitations into the document.
   - **Additional instruction for summary**: Focus on how to support V instructions in QEMU 1.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Hybrid simulation: QEMU (functional, MHz) + vsim (cycle-accurate Verilator+SystemC, KHz) sharing state
   - Three-phase round trip: QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2
   - `struct hybrid_state_v1` wire format (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)
   - Magic CSR trigger: `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (enter), `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit); plus runtime `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` and PC-trigger mode
   - RISC-V Debug Module: Abstract Commands + Program Buffer
   - Tier A V-CSR restore (Abstract w/ PB fallback): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
   - Tier B V-CSR restore (PB only): vsetvl x0, x5, x6 atomically restores vtype+vl
   - V regfile transport: shared scratch region at `kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`, fixture uses vle8.v/vse8.v
   - Passthrough optimization: vsim only re-reads x1..x31, PC, V-CSRs; FP/M-CSRs/PMP/V-regfile passthrough from QEMU 1 snapshot
   - QEMU TCG plugin API + gdbstub `-S` for state seeding
   - Shared mmap RAM at `/dev/shm/...`
   - HTIF magic-mem syscalls (`0xE0080010`) - vsim only
   - DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR (`0xE0080004`) - vsim console
   - RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`) - QEMU only when `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`
   - SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (0x20), SYS_WRITE0 (0x04)
   - libgloss/newlib stubs, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c
   - mstatus.VS=Initial / FS=Initial set in crt0
   - QEMU plugin's PC readback lag for V instructions (`qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")` issue)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`** (heavily edited)
     - Main doc rewritten multiple times. Final structure:
       1. Intro / Problem
       2. High-Level Concept (5 ideas)
       3. State Categories and Their Transport (table with passthrough column)
       4. Trigger Modes (CSR vs PC; ENTER/EXIT/RUNTIME)
       5. Design Rationale (passthrough, Tier A/B, Abstract+PB fallback, missing-register tolerance)
       6. Architecture Flowchart (mermaid)
       7. Walk-Through `rt_c_v_regs`
       8. Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For (8 rules + where each rule lives)
       9. Known Limitations (L1-L7) - **most recently added**
       10. Where to Look Next
       11. Mental Model

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`**
     - Wire format with vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[32*64], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, plus 13 M-mode CSRs, 16 pmpcfg + 64 pmpaddr, 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, PC

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`**
     - `ResumeDriver::resume()`: only writes GPRs, DPC, mtvec, and (when V) Tier-A V CSRs + Tier-B vsetvl + mstatus.VS. M-mode CSRs not written.
     - `write_csr_abstract_or_pb` - Abstract-first with PB fallback using x5 scratch.

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`**
     - `drain_with_passthrough(input)`: re-reads only x1..x31, PC, and (when V) the 6 V CSRs; everything else passthrough.

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`**
     - `set_passthrough_input` mechanism; on_retire decodes hit kind and fires drain.

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`**
     - `apply_state` sends GPRs (G packet), FPRs, 13 M-mode CSRs (kCsrByName), 80 PMP slots, V CSRs, V regfile via gdbstub P packets. Spawns QEMU 2 with `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`.

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`** (CRITICAL for next step)
     - `on_handoff`: reads `s.pc = read_reg64(v->pc)` via `qemu_plugin_read_register`. **This is the source of L2 limitation - lags for V instructions.**
     - `vcpu_tb_trans`: registers per-insn callback with kind as userdata.
     - Proposed fix:
     ```c
     // In vcpu_tb_trans:
     uintptr_t arg = ((uintptr_t)kind) | ((uintptr_t)qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) << 8);
     qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb(insn, on_handoff,
                                            QEMU_PLUGIN_CB_R_REGS, (void*)arg);
     // In on_handoff:
     hybrid_handoff_kind kind = (uintptr_t)udata & 0xff;
     uint64_t trigger_vaddr   = (uintptr_t)udata >> 8;
     s.pc = trigger_vaddr + 4;   // csrwi is always 4-byte
     ```

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/`**
     - `rt_c.ld`: load at 0x80000000, _stack=0x80700000
     - `crt0.S`: enables mstatus.FS=Initial (1<<13) and mstatus.VS=Initial (1<<10), sp/gp init, .bss clear, init_mtvec, main, _exit
     - `trap.S`: m_trap_entry saves x1, x3..x31, mepc, mstatus, mxstatus -> handle_trap
     - `handler.c`: ECALL -> syscall_table dispatch; else exception_handler
     - `libgloss.c`: HTIF stubs (TOHOST_CMD_REG = 0xE0080010); _write(fd<=2) -> DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR (0xE0080004); _exit uses semihosting magic triple with SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (0x20)

file.content:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory only. No V instructions here -- the
   * plugin's gdbstub-pc readback misreports the drain PC when V is in
   * flight, which would resume vsim at the wrong instruction. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

content.content:
1	/* Drain markers and fail-fast helper shared by all C-level round-trip
2	 * fixtures (rt_c_hello.c, rt_c_printf.c, rt_c_file_io.c).
3	 *
4	 * The fixtures are timing-aware: only the span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain()
5	 * and rt_phase_vsim_drain() may touch libgloss / sim_control MMIO. Outside
6	 * that window the program runs on QEMU virt, which has no peripheral at
7	 * 0xE0080000.
8	 */
9	
10	#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
11	#define RT_C_HELPERS_H
12	
13	#include <stdint.h>
14	#include <stdio.h>
15	
16	/* Matches src/platform/sim_control.hpp:32 SimCommand::FAILED. */
17	#define SIM_CONTROL_REG (*(volatile uint32_t*) 0xE0080000)
18	#define SIM_FAILED 0x01234569u
19	
20	/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
21	 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
22	 *
23	 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
24	 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
25	 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
26	 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
27	static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
28	{
29	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
30	                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
31	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
32	}
33	static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
34	{
35	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
36	                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
37	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
38	}
39	
40	/* Vsim-only failure path: writes SimCommand::FAILED so vsim's
41	 * handle_sim_command (sim_control.hpp:188) sets exits=1 and the simulator
42	 * loop returns 1 before the handoff fires. The harness sees RC=1 vs
43	 * EXPECT_RC=0 and reports a failure. Bypasses QEMU2 entirely. */
44	__attribute__((noreturn)) static inline void rt_fail(void)
45	{
46	  fflush(stdout);
47	  SIM_CONTROL_REG = SIM_FAILED;
48	  for (;;) {
49	  }
50	}
51	
52	/* RISC-V semihosting calls. Only safe in the QEMU2 phase (after
53	 * rt_phase_vsim_drain): QEMU is spawned with -semihosting-config
54	 * enable=on,target=native (qemu_handback.hpp:131-132) so the magic
55	 * slli/ebreak/srai triple is intercepted before reaching mtvec. vsim
56	 * does NOT implement semihosting, so executing these inside the vsim
57	 * span would route to m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loop. */
58	static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
59	{
60	  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
61	  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
62	  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
63	                       ".option norvc\n"
64	                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
65	                       "ebreak\n"
66	                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
67	                       ".option pop\n"
68	                       :
69	                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
70	                       : "memory");
71	}
72	
73	#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */
74

file.content:
csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \

---
# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so

108	  endif()
109	  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
110	
111	  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
112	  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
113	  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
114	  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
115	  # from the host's build/.
116	  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
117	    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
118	    "rt_pc_jump:0"
119	    "rt_sign_bits:0"
120	    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
121	    "rt_long_roi:0"
122	    "rt_all_gprs:0"
123	    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
124	    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
125	    "rt_csr_survival:0"
126	  )
127	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
128	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
129	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
130	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
131	    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
132	    add_test(
133	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
134	      COMMAND bash -c
135	        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
136	    )
