145	    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
146	    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
147	    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
148	    add_test(
149	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
150	      COMMAND bash -c
151	        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
152	    )
153	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
154	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
155	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
156	      COST 30
157	    )
158	  endforeach()
159	
160	  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
161	  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
162	  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
163	  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
164	  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
165	  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
166	  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
167	    "rt_shared_mem:0"
168	    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
169	    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
170	    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
171	    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
172	    "rt_c_hello:0"
173	    "rt_c_printf:0"
174	    "rt_c_file_io:0"
175	    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
176	    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
177	    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
178	    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
179	    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
180	    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
181	    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
182	    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
183	  )
184	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
185	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
186	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
187	    list(GET parts 1 erc)

192	      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
193	      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
194	      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
195	      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
196	      set(tcost ${tsec})
197	    endif()
198	    # CSR variant.
199	    add_test(
200	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
201	      COMMAND bash -c
202	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
203	    )
204	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
205	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
206	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
207	      COST ${tcost}
208	    )
209	    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.

214	    )

216	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77

218	      COST ${tcost}

220	  endforeach()
221	
222	  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
223	  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
224	  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
225	  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
226	  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

content.text:
# Plan: support V instructions in QEMU 1 (plugin patch)

## Root cause (recap)

`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:199` reads PC via `read_reg64(v->pc)` from inside the per-insn exec callback. TCG defers architectural-PC updates for performance; the gdbstub readback returns a stale PC when V insns retire just before the trigger. Stale PC -> vsim resumes at the wrong insn -> divergence. GPRs/FPRs/V regs don't share the bug (stored in `CPUArchState` directly, no TCG laziness).

## Fix

Replace the runtime PC read with a translation-time capture. `qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn)` and `qemu_plugin_insn_size(insn)` are deterministic per-translation. Pass `{kind, vaddr, size}` as the exec-callback userdata; in the callback compute `s.pc = vaddr + size`.

## Patch shape (~30 LoC, plugin-only)

```c
// hybrid_handoff.c
typedef struct {
    hybrid_handoff_kind kind;
    uint64_t            vaddr;
    uint32_t            size;
} HandoffSite;

// vcpu_tb_trans (both CSR-mode and PC-mode):
HandoffSite *site = g_new0(HandoffSite, 1);
site->kind  = kind;                              // ENTER for PC-mode
site->vaddr = qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn);
site->size  = qemu_plugin_insn_size(insn);
qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb(
    insn, on_handoff, QEMU_PLUGIN_CB_R_REGS, site);

// on_handoff:
const HandoffSite *site = udata;
if (site->kind != HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER) return;
...
s.pc = site->vaddr + site->size;
```

`g_new0` sites are never freed; bounded by trigger-site count (1-2 per fixture). Same lifetime model the plugin already uses for `outfile_path`.

## TDD ordering (Tidy First)

**S0 - structural**: extract `HandoffSite` + `compute_handoff_pc(const HandoffSite*)` into `include/hybrid/handoff_site.h` (parallel to `insn_match.h`). No behavior change yet (plugin still encodes kind-only in udata, helper unused). Commit: `structural:`.

**S1 - RED**: add `tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp` (doctest, registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` next to `test_insn_match`). Cover: `vaddr+4` for 4-byte trigger, `vaddr+2` for 2-byte (PC-mode compressed-insn case), kind passthrough. Test fails (helper exists but plugin doesn't use it yet -- not testable directly; instead test the helper contract).

**S2 - GREEN**: switch `vcpu_tb_trans` to allocate `HandoffSite`, switch `on_handoff` to use `compute_handoff_pc`. Helper test now passes; e2e tests must still pass unchanged (golden path: `bash run_e2e.sh JOBS=8`). Commit: `behavioral:`.

**S3 - integration RED -> GREEN**: extend `rt_c_v_regs.c` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) with a small V seed sequence (e.g., `vsetvli` + `vle32.v` to load SEED into v8) before `rt_phase_qemu1_drain()`. Pre-S2 this would fail (stale PC); post-S2 it passes. Commit: `behavioral:`.

**S4 - doc**: in `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`, drop L2 from "Known Limitations" and update the comment block at the top of `rt_c_v_regs.c` (lines 4-5, 55-57). Commit: `structural:` (doc-only).

## Test strategy

| Layer | What it pins | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| `handoff_site.test.cpp` (doctest) | `compute_handoff_pc` returns `vaddr+size`; kind preserved | ~30 LoC |
| `rt_c_v_regs` extended Phase 1 | end-to-end: V insns in QEMU 1 don't desync PC | ~5 asm lines |
| Existing e2e regression suite | no other fixture broke | already wired |

Run: `ctest -L hybrid-e2e -j8` inside the vsim build container, plus `bash run_e2e.sh JOBS=8` on the host for the full Tier-2 suite.

## Open questions / risks

1. **PC-mode compressed-insn generalisation**: today PC-mode lacks a size guard (plugin comment line 270-273); with `site->size` from the API, this is no longer a latent bug. Worth calling out in commit message.
2. **Other stale reads?** The `rt_c_v_regs.c` comment singles out PC. If QEMU 2 mismatches surface in GPR/FPR/V values once V is allowed in QEMU 1, the fix becomes invasive (force CPUArchState sync before reads). Out of scope for this patch; gate it on actual evidence.
3. **TB invalidation**: each re-translation of a trigger TB allocates a fresh site. Fine for fixture workloads; revisit if SMC fixtures appear.

## Files touched

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `include/hybrid/handoff_site.h` | new (struct + helper) |
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | use `HandoffSite` in trans + on_handoff |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp` | new doctest |
| `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | register the new test |
| `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c` | add V seed sequence in Phase 1 |
| `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` | drop L2 |

Want me to execute S0 first?

# ##############################################################################

enable_testing()

function(vsim_add_test TARGET SOURCE)
  add_executable(${TARGET} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL ${SOURCE})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE ${VSIM_INCLUDE_DIRS})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src/utils")
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/external/doctest/doctest")
  target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
  target_compile_definitions(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS})
  target_link_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_LINK_FLAGS})
  add_test(NAME ${TARGET} COMMAND $<TARGET_FILE:${TARGET}>)
endfunction()

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1

{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to

   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory only. No V instructions here -- the
   * plugin's gdbstub-pc readback misreports the drain PC when V is in
   * flight, which would resume vsim at the wrong instruction. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 * Goal: prove that `vle32.v ... vN` issued in QEMU1 lands in vsim with the
 * same bytes; that vsim's modifications survive into QEMU2; and that the
 * QEMU2-side `vse32.v` re-derives the expected pattern.
 *
 *   QEMU1: load v8..v15 from a deterministic int32 pattern via __riscv_vle32.
 *   vsim:  set v9 = v8 + 100 elementwise (modify exactly one V reg).
 *   QEMU2: store v8..v15 back to memory + verify:
 *     v8  unchanged from seed
 *     v9  == seed + 100
 *     v10..v15 unchanged from seed
 *
 * Memory layout (matches rt_c_matmul.c convention): fixed-address scratch
 * region above the ELF's last loaded byte (~0x80003000) and below the stack
 * (0x80700000). Survives both QEMU's -kernel load and vsim's elf_loader
 * zero-fill because nothing inside [0x80100000, 0x80104000) belongs to a
 * PT_LOAD segment.
 *
 * vlen contract: this fixture assumes VLEN=512 (vlenb=64, 16 int32 per
 * register). QEMU is launched with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
 * tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh) and vsim is built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <riscv_vector.h>
#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */

246:## Known Limitations

content.content:
200	`v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes.
201	
202	`rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example.
203	
204	### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains
205	
206	ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.
207	
208	### 5. Don't run V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain
209	
210	The TCG plugin reads PC at retire of `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`; when a V insn is in flight immediately beforehand, the readback misreports the drain PC and vsim resumes at the wrong instruction. Pattern: keep Phase A scalar (seed memory with `sw`/`sd`, set up GPRs); do all V work inside the vsim window. `rt_c_v_regs.c` calls this out in its header comment.
211	
212	### 6. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`
213	
214	The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it.
215	
216	### 7. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window
217	
218	Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts:
219	
220	- `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`).
221	- `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`).
222	- `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code).
223	- Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`.
224	
225	Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim.
226	
227	### 8. Microarchitectural state does not transfer
228	
229	Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.
230	
231	### Where each rule lives in the runtime
232	
233	| Concern                                         | File                                           |
234	|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
235	| Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address)      | `runtime/rt_c.ld`                              |
236	| `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear      | `runtime/crt0.S`                               |
237	| M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch         | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
238	| HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`)       |
239	| Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit`                    |
240	| Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...)      | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
241	| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |
242	| QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` |
243	
244	For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.
245	
246	## Known Limitations
247	
248	These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list.
249	
250	### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped
251	
252	**What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind.
253	
254	**Affected registers:**
255	- All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`).
256	- 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped.
257	- All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`).
258	- `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target).
259	
260	**Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP.
261	
262	**Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase.
263	
264	**Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block.
265	
266	### L2. No V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain marker
267	
268	**What this means.** Phase 1 must stay scalar. Setting up V regfile contents or V-CSRs in QEMU 1 is fine in principle (the plugin drains all of them), but if the *last instruction before* `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` is a V instruction, vsim resumes at the wrong PC.
269	
270	**Why.** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c::on_handoff` reads the drain PC by calling `qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")`. QEMU's gdbstub-backed view of `env->pc` can lag for V helper-implemented instructions, so the recorded `s.pc` does not point at the instruction immediately after the marker.
271	
272	**Workaround.** Either (a) insert a `fence rw, rw` plus a couple of `nop`s between the last V insn and the marker, or (b) seed V state in Phase 1 only via memory (write to addresses that Phase 2 will `vle*.v` from), keeping all V instructions inside the vsim window.
273	
274	**Permanent fix.** Compute the drain PC from `qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) + 4` (`csrwi` is always 4-byte) at translate time and stash it in the per-callback userdata. Five-line change in the plugin.
275	
276	### L3. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff
277	
278	Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording.
279	
280	### L4. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs
281	
282	The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug.
283	
284	### L5. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined
285	
286	Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone.
287	
288	### L6. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported
289	
290	The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it.
291	
292	### L7. Single hart only (today)
293	
294	The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end.
295	
296	## Where to Look Next
297	
298	- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract.
299	- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback.

`v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes.

`rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example.

### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains

ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop.

### 5. Don't run V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain

The TCG plugin reads PC at retire of `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`; when a V insn is in flight immediately beforehand, the readback misreports the drain PC and vsim resumes at the wrong instruction. Pattern: keep Phase A scalar (seed memory with `sw`/`sd`, set up GPRs); do all V work inside the vsim window. `rt_c_v_regs.c` calls this out in its header comment.

### 6. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`

The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it.

### 7. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window

Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts:

- `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`).
- `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`).
- `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code).
- Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`.

Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim.

### 8. Microarchitectural state does not transfer

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.

### Where each rule lives in the runtime

| Concern                                         | File                                           |
|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address)      | `runtime/rt_c.ld`                              |
| `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear      | `runtime/crt0.S`                               |
| M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch         | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
| HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`)       |
| Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit`                    |
| Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...)      | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |
| QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` |

For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-8 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

## Known Limitations

These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list.

### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped

**What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind.

**Affected registers:**
- All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`).
- 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped.
- All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`).
- `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target).

**Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP.

**Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase.

**Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block.

### L2. No V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain marker

**What this means.** Phase 1 must stay scalar. Setting up V regfile contents or V-CSRs in QEMU 1 is fine in principle (the plugin drains all of them), but if the *last instruction before* `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` is a V instruction, vsim resumes at the wrong PC.

**Why.** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c::on_handoff` reads the drain PC by calling `qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")`. QEMU's gdbstub-backed view of `env->pc` can lag for V helper-implemented instructions, so the recorded `s.pc` does not point at the instruction immediately after the marker.

**Workaround.** Either (a) insert a `fence rw, rw` plus a couple of `nop`s between the last V insn and the marker, or (b) seed V state in Phase 1 only via memory (write to addresses that Phase 2 will `vle*.v` from), keeping all V instructions inside the vsim window.

**Permanent fix.** Compute the drain PC from `qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) + 4` (`csrwi` is always 4-byte) at translate time and stash it in the per-callback userdata. Five-line change in the plugin.

### L3. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording.

### L4. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs

The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug.

### L5. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined

Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone.

### L6. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported

The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it.

### L7. Single hart only (today)

The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end.

## Where to Look Next

- `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract.
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback.

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them.

input.old_string:
### L2. No V instructions in QEMU 1 before the entry drain marker

**What this means.** Phase 1 must stay scalar. Setting up V regfile contents or V-CSRs in QEMU 1 is fine in principle (the plugin drains all of them), but if the *last instruction before* `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` is a V instruction, vsim resumes at the wrong PC.

**Why.** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c::on_handoff` reads the drain PC by calling `qemu_plugin_read_register("pc")`. QEMU's gdbstub-backed view of `env->pc` can lag for V helper-implemented instructions, so the recorded `s.pc` does not point at the instruction immediately after the marker.

**Workaround.** Either (a) insert a `fence rw, rw` plus a couple of `nop`s between the last V insn and the marker, or (b) seed V state in Phase 1 only via memory (write to addresses that Phase 2 will `vle*.v` from), keeping all V instructions inside the vsim window.

**Permanent fix.** Compute the drain PC from `qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr(insn) + 4` (`csrwi` is always 4-byte) at translate time and stash it in the per-callback userdata. Five-line change in the plugin.

### L3. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff

Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording.

The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it.

 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory (no V instructions; the
 *          QEMU plugin's per-insn callback misreports the drained PC when
 *          the immediately-preceding code uses V).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

75:## Architecture Flowchart
124:## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test
133:### What each phase does
144:### State that crosses each handoff
152:### What each step proves
163:## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For
165:You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists.
167:### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase
187:### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation
198:### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates
204:### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains
208:### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi`
212:### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window
223:### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer
227:### Where each rule lives in the runtime
240:For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.
242:## Known Limitations
244:These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list.
246:### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped
262:### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff
266:### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs
270:### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined
274:### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported
278:### L6. Single hart only (today)
282:## Where to Look Next
297:## Mental Model in One Sentence

input.command:
git add include/hybrid/handoff_site.h && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
structural: add HandoffSite helper for translation-time PC capture

The QEMU TCG plugin currently reads the trigger PC at exec-callback time via
qemu_plugin_read_register("pc"); TCG defers PC writes for performance, so
that readback can lag the actual current insn when V instructions retire
just before the marker. Capture vaddr+size at vcpu_tb_trans time instead
(both are deterministic per-translation) and compute the post-trigger PC
from those.

This commit only adds the header + helper. The plugin still uses the
runtime PC read; that swap is a separate behavioral change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

input.command:
cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim && git add qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: plugin captures trigger PC via translation-time HandoffSite

Replace the runtime qemu_plugin_read_register("pc") path with a
HandoffSite allocated at vcpu_tb_trans time. The exec callback now
computes s.pc from the captured vaddr+size, eliminating a class of
stale-PC bugs when V instructions retire just before the trigger
(TCG defers PC writes; the gdbstub readback observes the lag).

Side effect: PC-mode now uses qemu_plugin_insn_size(insn) for next-PC,
removing the latent assumption that enter_pc parks on a 4-byte insn.
Compressed-insn enter_pc is now correctly handled.

Sites are heap-allocated and never freed; bounded by trigger-site
count in the binary (typically 1-2 csrwi insns).

Verified: all 36 e2e fixtures pass under JOBS=8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Done. The pre-existing `external/systemc-cci` dirt is unrelated and stays untouched.

## Summary

**Outer repo (`main`)** — 4 new commits:
- `b3525d7 structural:` add `include/hybrid/handoff_site.h`
- `96002bb behavioral:` plugin captures trigger PC via `HandoffSite` (no more `read_reg64(v->pc)`)
- `f9ed5fa structural:` drop V-in-QEMU-1 rule 5 + L2, renumber remaining entries
- `a06823b structural:` bump submodule

**Submodule (`hybrid_vsim` / `hybrid`)** — 2 new commits:
- `9551058 structural:` register `test_handoff_site` doctest
- `9f7031e behavioral:` exercise V insns in `rt_c_v_regs` Phase 1

**Verification:** unit test compiles+runs (3/3 doctest assertions pass) and full host e2e is 36/36 across csr+pc modes including the new V-in-Phase-1 sequence. The patch also incidentally fixes the latent compressed-insn gap in PC-mode (next-PC now uses `qemu_plugin_insn_size` instead of an implicit "+4").

**Note:** I couldn't reproduce the original stale-PC failure with the unpatched plugin in current QEMU 9.x — both variants pass V-in-Phase-1. The patch is therefore a defensive correctness fix (translation-time data is strictly more reliable than the gdbstub PC readback) rather than a regression repro. The historical limitation comment is gone either way.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

lines: # Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
-RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
+RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

)
